system.description:
Medieval medicine, especially in the Christian kingdoms, is not very advanced, basing all its diagnoses and treatments on the old studies of Roman and Greek doctors; especially Galen, whose theory of the equilibrium of the four humors in the body dominates all medicine until the Modern Age is well underway. The Jews and Arabs, however, know techniques for curatives and diagnosis much more advanced than the Christians.

This skill represents the character's level of knowledge about medicine and anatomy, permitting him to diagnose and cure illnesses, wounds, or poisonings, as long as adequate medical equipment is at his disposal — medicines, enemas, leeches, bandages — as well as time and rest on the part of the patient.
